My experience with Sesame. My experience with Sesame, particularly regarding prescription issues involving the Walgreens pharmacy on Elliot Road in Tempe, has been frustrating. 

Before last month, I was looking into joining the weight loss program through Sesame. I completed the weight loss questionnaire, after which I was prompted to enter payment information and subscribe to Sesame Plus for $10 per month, which I believed was required in order to participate in the program. However, when I met with the provider, I was told that they were not associated with the weight loss program and that a refund would be issued. I then tried booking another appointment, but I had to call Sesame to confirm that this appointment also was not part of the weight loss program. That appointment had to be canceled, and the agent I spoke with directed me to the correct subscription, called Success by Sesame. Only after enrolling in the proper program was I able to be seen for a Wegovy prescription.

Last month, I also had problems with my prescription. It was initially sent to Walmart Pharmacy, but Walmart told me they had never received the prescription. Because the issue could not be resolved, the prescription eventually had to be transferred to Walgreens. I still had to follow up multiple times and go back and forth trying to determine whether it had actually been sent and what the out-of-pocket cost would be. It took a significant amount of time and effort just to ensure I would be able to get my medication.

This month, my prescription was initially sent to Walgreens. I was unable to see the price on the Sesame website, unlike the first month, (at which time my first prescription also came with a GoodRX coupon by default without going to the manufacturer's website to redeem the manufacturer reduced price, which wasn't the case this time), so I called Walgreens and was told that my out-of-pocket cost would be over $1,000. I declined and had the prescription transferred to CVS instead.

I then went to the manufacturer's website and obtained a coupon that reduced my out-of-pocket cost to $200. After CVS began giving me problems, I decided to transfer the prescription back to Walgreens. Before doing so, I called Walgreens to confirm whether they would accept the coupon, and they assured me that it would bring the cost down to $200. However, Walgreens later told me they never received the prescription after it had been sent from CVS. Even after the prescription was resent to Walgreens, I was told the same thing, and the issue remained unresolved. I then had to contact Sesame again to see what was going on. Sesame told me they had contacted the pharmacy and that I could pick it up at 3 p.m. on 6/17/26. I wanted to call to check on the status with the pharmacy, but I was on hold for a while. After finally getting through, they confirmed that they had received the prescription, that my out-of-pocket cost would be $200, and when it would be ready. Only then was the issue finally resolved.

As a result, I spent hours trying to locate my medication and coordinate between the pharmacies and my provider. I expected clearer communication and better coordination from Sesame. Instead, I experienced confusion, inconsistent information, numerous phone calls, and unnecessary stress.

I am tired of having to jump through hoops to get basic issues resolved, which is why I canceled Sesame and will not be using their service again. The lack of coordination and support throughout this process only added to the frustration

Felt like a total scam. Avoid.. I booked Sesame to get a prescription for B12 injections, which is a routine request, with a documented history of low B12 that I explained to their "physician" up front. He never asked to see a single lab. Never reviewed my records. Never engaged with any specific of my case. He issued a blanket refusal before looking at anything.

What's more, this "physician" seemed to not understand basic human biology, and the two reasons he gave were both flatly wrong. First, that B12 is "a GI issue" needing a gastroenterologist — it isn't; B12 injections for deficiency and pernicious anemia are routine primary care that GPs handle every day. Second, that he couldn't "verify or modulate" the dose — there is nothing to modulate; injectable B12 comes in standardized ampoules at fixed, well-established doses. B12 has no known toxic dose and is sold over the counter without a prescription in many different countries for this reason.

So, TLDR, I paid for an individualized medical assessment and received a pre-formed "no" from someone who didn't read my file. When I asked for a refund, Sesame refused. In my opinion that is indistinguishable from a scam: charge for a consultation, deliver nothing of clinical value, then keep the money. I would not use them again and would warn anyone considering it to spend their money somewhere a provider will actually look at your case

All were within 2 hrs as they promised.  Care/treatment was acceptable.  Unfortunately, March 2024, she needed xrays while she was at a Memory Care facility.  She is now 96 and has dementia.  She fell and hurt her back, and I didn't want her to spend numerous hours in ER alone as I was out of town.  The DispatchHealth/PPX Imaging couldn't give the facility an ETA (which apparently was common), saying they were short-staffed. I called the facility 24 hours later thinking the imaging operated the same as the urgent care part of DispatchHealth, without knowing that it was common for the x-ray techs to come 2 or more days later.  I was surprised that my aunt was not seen, no ETA of x-ray tech.  I called DispatchHealth, they did not have a record of the order, said I had to call DispatchHealth/PPX directly.  I did.  They tried to again kick the can down the road and wanted to give me the third-party scheduling company.  I refused and said it's their job to follow up.  I told them my aunt was still in pain and if they couldn't get there within a reasonable amount of time, they should have said that in the beginning, then I would have insisted she go to the ER.  The facility staff also again called for ETA to no avail and was told they were on the other side of town 40 plus mins away doing x-rays there.  Finally that evening, approx. 36 hrs or more later, they came and did the x-ray, but only because we followed up and complained about the lack of triage, lack of protocol for assessing patients and especially because of lack of staff.  I will not use the x-ray portion of DispatchHealth unless it's not urgent or the patient is not in pain.  Terrible disregard for the age of the patient, seemed to care more about the tech's travel time

I really could not be more pleased. I really could not be more pleased. I was vacationing with family in Del Mar, CA. I had experienced ringing in my ear for about a week - and while in Del Mar, woke up feeling like the ear was badly plugged. I didn't want to spend my vacation finding an urgent care. Contacted Dispatch Health - I honestly thought it was a scam at first. Set the appointment, gave my credit card info, etc. Shockingly, received a text in about 45 mins saying the team was already on the way. A competent PA and two medical staffers were at my hotel within the hour. The PA checked both ears and determined that the one that hurt was clogged with wax, as she couldn't see the ear drum. All three staffers then participated in irrigating my ear multiple times. Forgive the disgusting details, but a gigantic ball of wax finally dislodged and fell out of my ear. It was absolutely disgusting - but the ringing reduced, the pain was completely gone, and my hearing has never been better during my adult life (lol). I was so impressed with the service, the protocol, and how proactive they were to identify the issue and fix it. If you need a mobile health visit, don't hesitate to call these good people. They also have a good arsenal of equipment, so I'm confident they can handle most urgent care issues. The staffers who visited were Bobbie, Erin, and Victoria - they were excellent!

These are the true heroes. I'll never forgot how they cared for my Mom. Keep up the great work. I will recommend you to all my friends and family

Dangerously incompetent care program – shocking lack of accountability. Included Health’s new care program is a serious downgrade that puts infants at risk. What used to be access to a pediatrician has been quietly replaced with registered nurses, and the consequences are alarming.
For our one-month-old baby, a nurse advised us to wait 48 hours instead of seeking urgent care. For a newborn. This advice was reckless and completely inappropriate. Had we listened, the outcome could have been disastrous. We ignored the guidance, went to urgent care, and confirmed that waiting would have been a mistake.
To make matters worse, I escalated the issue and spoke with Jennifer from the Member Resolution Team. Rather than acknowledging the severity of the situation, the response felt dismissive and defensive, as if this level of care was acceptable. It is not. A “resolution team” that cannot recognize how dangerous this advice was has no credibility.
Downgrading pediatric care to nurse-only check-ins without clearly warning parents is irresponsible. Parents trust this service during stressful, high-stakes moments — especially with newborns — and Included Health has shown that trust is not deserved.
This program is unsafe, poorly managed, and completely misaligned with basic standards of pediatric care. We will never use this service again, and I strongly warn other parents: do not rely on Included Health for infant medical decisions

Incompetence and discriminatory behavior.. They spent half month looking for a provider that treats autism / ADHD / PTSD for an adult male...  They pat themselves on the back during the process giving you this false sense that they are going to find you the ultimate in care.  On their app while you're waiting it says they're using billions of data points to calculate the best possible provider..   so what did they find in autistic man a half month later? Which by the way I have the screenshots to prove it. 

They found me a gynecologist... Yep...   After I pointed this out they did an investigation and apologized..  there's so many times that they failed. They sent me a email stating that my diagnosis is complex and difficult. And I am to no longer use their app. But only use their email.. 

And within that email I have a minimum of a 3-day wait for any replies instead of an instantaneous reply using the app.. then they limited my access to emails to 5 days a week. When all my other employees have 24/7 access..  the more they try to help me the more they exposed how truly incompetent and untrained they are..  but in the process of providing extreme limits on me. The state of Pennsylvania decided this was a discriminatory..  unequal access to care..  then they tried to cover their tracks by blocking my access to my HPI.. which had the proof that they found me a gynecologist.. m

But once again they violated laws and were reminded to reinstate my HPI or there will be consequences.. and so they did..  I've been with my company for 25 years. We've had many healthcare type services like this in the past. Included health is by far the most incompetent I have ever dealt with..    

When they deemed working with an autistic person to be difficult they try to make up rules in their terms of service limiting in my access to their care. This was mention to me in a letter... M

Once again, it was uncovered through an investigation that this terms of service limitation didn't even exist they created it to try to get a person with disabilities to not bother them. Because they legitimately did not know what they were doing....   Then they tried to blame me for not using their virtual care through the included health app. I had to remind them they do not provide autism related care. So after they were done blaming me for not taking advantage of the resources now they didn't want to talk about that anymore because they realized they just made a fool out of themselves in writing.. 

By blaming me for not taking advantage of virtual care that they don't even offer... They are so bad if I didn't have all this stuff in print. It would look like the biggest fabrication..  but the fact that I do have all this in print is why there is still a pending State investigation. 

They discriminated against me so badly that they introduced me to a new term I wasn't familiar with. Gaslighting. They would gaslight me and tell me they're not discriminating against me. And they're not limiting my access to their care.  As they put in writing that I am only allowed to contact them through email. As they are disabling the chat feature and the reasons for being this way is they do not have people clinically trained to help me through the app..  I also have screenshots of that statement.. 

The worst is when they lie to an autistic person with adhd. Gaslight them. Make them feel like a horrible person for needing help beyond what the average person needs. Labels them difficult and complex with their health care needs. And then makes it increasingly difficult and time consuming. Where in one situation they took 35 days to get back to me..  despite the fact that they said they're not limiting my access..  

At this point I told them to deactivate my account. The investigation is still going on. But I told them I will not allow you to treat me this way. I would rather get no care. Then dysfunctional care that resents that people like me exist..  I have so many screenshots of everything that they've said.  ... I never in my life imagine somebody with health in their name, looking at an autistic person with ADHD as a burden, as complex, as difficult, and as something they would rather not deal with

Rough onboarding now resolved after my employer engaged with Included Health. It would appear that what I outlined below in my initial review was a one to two week rough onboarding period as now I am able to reach member services and receive assistance.

--------------------
 
My employer engaged with Included Health after switching from Anthem (Blue Cross CA) to Blue Shield CA with Included Health as the “Member Services” layer between us (the insured) and BSCA.

It has been a disaster so far at only eight days in!

I have attempted to call them at the Member Service phone number three times in the last three days and have waited on hold with music for at least one hour each time before giving up.  One time I simultaneously started a Member Services chat on their web portal and no human joined the chat from their end for the entire hour.

All I can say is that since it has been impossible to reach them, how can I even begin to believe that they will help me with my needs?

The marketing on transition to them was that they would provide excellent assistance in navigating the complex health care needs of the members.

Give me a break…

Now there is simply extra complexity and wasting time just trying to talk to them so that I can explain the “complex health care needs” that they might simplify for me
TOTAL waste of time. TOTAL waste of time. POOR service

No added value, and their service keeps worsening over time. My insurance used to include a benefit called Grand Rounds, where a team would recommend doctors and schedule appointments for me. Unfortunately, ever since they rebranded as Included Health, they got rid of this concierge service, and now just provide lists of doctors (sometimes inaccurate), which I could have gotten just by going to my insurance website myself.

Worse than that, I'm forced to get my mental health care through Included Health's Doctor on Demand video call feature, which is a nightmare when their website and app keep crashing right before a scheduled video call. I logged in 20 minutes before a call today but the website provided no way to log in to the call, and in fact erased my entire video call history and care team.

They used to provide a customer service phone number, but that's vanished from the website too; looks like they laid off a lot of their customer service reps. Instead I had to go through an AI chatbot, which told me I would get a response "within 24 hours." Not helpful when my scheduled call was in 15 minutes.

I only barely avoided the $95 no-show fee by downloading the app at the last minute.

But after the call, the app would crash every single time I tried to book a follow-up visit with my doctor. And the website still removed every possible way to book a follow-up visit with him. So I have to wait until they fix their app or website to see my doctor, I guess

Don't Provide Claimed Service, Spammers. Their service is unhelpful and they are incorrigible spammers. They are offered as a free benefit through my husband's employer, so we decided to try them. They referred us to random doctors in our area that specifically violated one of the critera we outlined (more on this below). Since asking them to close my account, they periodically spam me with marketing emails in spite of my now repeated efforts to unsubscribe and ask them to cease from resubscribing me. They ignore emails for support on this topic. So: they provide a useless service then become spammers.

Regarding their referrals: my husband and I (both gay men who do not have to worry about pregnancy) refuse to use doctors affilatied with the St. Joseph Health system (recently rebranded Providence) because of their abortion policies. We communicated this when trying their referral service. Even under life-threatening, St. Joseph does not permit abortions in their hospitals, and they do not allow doctors to refer patients for abortions. We will not support any health system them puts religious views over healtcare or any patient's life. Included Health referred both of us to different doctors affiliated with this system (my husband didn't find out until his gastroenterologist, who had a separate office outside of the hospital, schedule a colonoscopy at St. Joseph Hospital where he also practices). I discovered the affiliation of the doctor they referred me to before scheduling, and I sent them links to his profile on the St. Joseph website, but in spite of this evidence, they proceeded to try to gaslight me about his affiliation. In both cases, these doctors were just one of the first to pop up in the list of in-network providers on our insururer's websites and had no particular claims to being LGBT friently suggesting they put in no dilligence at all in their referrals

Data breach. I live the US. I received housekeeping applicants who live in Mexico! Then, within hours, I started getting charges on my credit card made by people living in Mexico! Serious data breach and very, very poor screening. 
1) Responded to an applicant for my housekeeping job after signing up for Care.com and paying with my credit card. 
2) Applicant did not respond for two days, the messaged that she was in Mexico, she was sorry for the delay.
3) Less than 24 hours later my credit card began being used in Mexico - to open a Netflix account, make purchases on Amazon.com, etc.
4) I contacted Netflix when I received notice of a new account. I had them close the account in Mexico.
5) After checking which charges on my card that were fraudulent, I cancelled my credit card used on Care.com and notified the credit card company.
6) I do not know anyone in Mexico. This was all too coincidental. Open care.com on day 1, respond to applicant on day 2 then says she is in Mexico on day 4, charges begin appearing on my credit card early morning day 5

No CS, Charged Immediately, Never Used the Service, Refund Refused. I rarely leave negative reviews, but parents should be aware of my experience with Care.com.

I signed up looking for childcare assistance and was immediately charged for a membership and additional fees that were not clearly disclosed during the sign-up process. As soon as I noticed the charges, I contacted Care.com, canceled my account, and requested a refund.

I never used the service. I did not contact caregivers, post a job, exchange messages, or receive any benefit from the membership. Despite canceling immediately and never using the service, Care.com refused to issue a refund.

What made the situation even more frustrating was how difficult it was to get help. It was nearly impossible to speak with a live representative, and my concerns were repeatedly dismissed.

As a single parent, I find it incredibly disappointing that a company serving families would refuse a refund under these circumstances. I made every effort to resolve the issue directly and in good faith.

Based on my experience, I cannot recommend Care.com and would encourage parents to carefully review all charges before signing up

I’m a certified Health Care Aide. I’m a certified Health Care Aide, and I make $18.50 an hour. When I look at how close that is to minimum wage — especially with minimum wage going up again in April — it’s hard not to feel the gap between the work I do and the value placed on it. I care for people at their most vulnerable moments, physically and emotionally, and I give my whole heart to this work, yet my wage doesn’t reflect the weight of that responsibility.

At Home Instead, there is also no paid overtime and no benefits, which makes that gap even more obvious. There was also a statement from the owner that any kilometres driven outside of Red Deer would be paid at 75 cents per kilometre, yet no one receives this either. Many Health Care Aides across Alberta are going through the same situation — doing essential, hands‑on care for wages that barely sit above minimum wage.

The office sees the hard work we do every day, from acute care to hospice care, yet the support we need isn’t reflected in wages, policies, or follow‑through. I previously worked in a hotel making $18 an hour just to make beds and clean — tasks I also do in this job — yet here I carry far more responsibility, emotional labour, and risk for nearly the same pay.

I care so deeply for my clients that it’s not in me to just walk away for a higher‑paying job. I don’t want my clients stressed or left wondering if I’m coming back. Many of them would ask over and over where I went, or worry that something was wrong. I could get a job with the City making close to $30 an hour just to take care of the outdoors, but my clients are number one to me — and that loyalty should matter.

Home Instead also has an Employee of the Month program, but because I work 12‑hour shifts Monday to Friday, I’m not eligible since I can’t work weekends. I work 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. just to cover my $3000 rent and bills, living paycheck to paycheck, needing a car to get to work, and trying to keep up with gas, food, and everything else life demands. On weekends, I clean houses so I can afford to put my daughter in softball. That level of commitment should be recognized, not excluded.

All of us employees need to stand together, and the office manager should be supporting their staff instead of leaving them to struggle alone. Being paid only slightly above minimum wage highlights a serious issue within the company and within the healthcare system as a whole: the people providing intimate, essential care are not compensated in a way that matches the skill, training, and emotional labour required. Sustainable, quality care depends on fair wages, fair policies, and fair treatment for frontline workers.

Shame on them - bereavement skills non existent!. The carers are wonderful, the admin staff and company are another story.  My father died 15 minutes before the night carer arrived. No message of condolence, or even an email or phone call acknowledging that he had died. No contact from them at all until we asked for a bill, and even then it was not an appropriate response which we were extremely disappointed by, we were flabbergasted to be honest. We dealt with the Bletchley / Milton Keynes office.  Shame on them!

HomeInstead North Wales bad experience.. Despite having an excellent experience with HomeInstead Chester for my elderly mother, we had to use a different franchise as she moved into Wales. If you are using this company PLEASE read the small print in your contract as not all franchise contracts will be the same.  Despite knowing my mother was in hospital and my keeping them fully informed they still insisted on charging her for full fees for the time she remained in hospital.  We were told we should be grateful that they had made the ‘generous’ offer of not charging for the full month and only 3 weeks! Full payment for doing absolutely nothing. A little heads up. A little heads up, what you can expect, from Home instead, if you go for an interview with them.
This was the Norfolk/Cambridge branch, in the UK.
   So, yesterday I had an online interview with Home Instead, and I thought it went fine, but obviously not, as I didn't get the job.
The young man, said that at the end of the day, he would contact me, he didn't. I had to chase up an answer today. 
It is what it is, not getting the job, that one has applied for, but the reasons given were so completely untrue and unfair. But that just shows, that if a company is  behaving like that, then it is of course, best not to work for them. 

The reasons they gave me was, that I didn't show interest in the work...I was shocked to hear that, as I most definitely showed great interest, in working with the elderly clients. I kept on asking questions, whether it was ok, to do gardening with the clients, and cooking, baking, painting, arts and crafts etc. 
I also asked for advice, in how to deal with challenging situations, if they should arise.
So, I did ask many questions about different scenarios, and also asked if I could do certain activities with them, such as gardening, baking, etc.
If that is not showing interest, and involvement in the job, I don't know what is.

Then they also mentioned, that being dressed in a hoodie, was not considered appropriate and unprofessional...Now this also shocked me, as my hoodie was down, it was obviously NOT over my head, it was down, so my whole head was visible, of course.
I was wearing a clean hoodie, casual relaxed clothing. For this line of work, I would have thought, that this was accepted, as you are to spend, a lot of time, with the elderly client in their homes, so being in comfortable clothing, is a must.
This is not a front of house position, in a corporate business, or in a Hotel. This is looking after elderly clients in their homes, for two weeks at a time, so relaxed clothing should definitely be accepted.
 But clearly it is not.

Another reason, was that I didn't have a fixed address...also not correct. I live in between my caravan, and my art workshop at the moment, while I am looking for a house. However, I have a postal address, where they can reach me, and most importantly, via my phone obviously. 
I will anyway, be away for two weeks at a time, with the client, so those reasons, are completely unfounded.

The only thing, that was close to resembling any truth, was that I didn't know much, about the company, other than it takes care, of elderly people.
 I find this to be so irrelevant, whether you know the whole Wikipedia, about the company, when it was created, every single detail, etc.
They work with elderly people, provide in-house services for them for two weeks at a time, that involving, cooking, gardening, walks in the park, shopping, etc. that should be enough to mention that, and I did.
You may know, all the bio about the company, but still not be a good carer, that doesn't ensure, that you are good at your job. It is about being good with people, and I am.
  it is some weird criteria, and very robotic, the way they judge, whether you are suitable for the position or not.

In this case, the reasons, they gave me, about why, I didn't get the job, was also very untrue and unfounded.

It seems like they are very incompetent rigid and soulless, no passion for engagement with people, nor do they seem to have any skill, in hiring people.
They seemed more interested in the job candidates, knowledge of the company logistics, and how they are dressed, rather than if the job candidate is a warm, caring person, who really would be good as a carer. 

Their reviews speak for themselves, they have 63% one-star reviews and less than 30%, five-star reviews, and that is from both relatives of the clients and people who have worked for them, or been in the onboarding process.
Cold and uncaring people. Would definitely NOT recommend Home instead.

So now you know, what you can expect, from having an interview with Home instead.

                     Karina

This is an update to a previous review…. This is an update to a previous review and one which I am saddened to write. I originally only wanted a one off 24 hours care. The person who I spoke to advised it was better to sign up for a package so the care giver assigned to mum whilst I was away would be familiar with her routine and mum would get to know her. This did not happen. One of The care givers assigned to mum I only met the day before I flew out and I never got to meet the second carer. This meant vital information was not passed on from the carer I met to the one I didn't. Please let me stress the carer I met was lovely and very nice and I know she did her best but mum is incredibly high needs. 98 years. End stage dementia not mobile and a catheter. I was assured at time of signing up that this company specialises in this type of care. Was my mother in mortal danger? No. Could my mothers health been compromised had I been away longer? I believe so. Again everything the carers did was with good intent and I'm sure they were kind and caring to mum but I firmly believe they were not equipped training wise to care for mum. Everything I have said here I have also emailed the company with this so my comments should not be a surprise. I also accept some responsibility. I was so exhausted and stressed I wanted to believe everything they said and I should have asked more questions. Billing was also an issue. I arrived home ,to a broken washing machine, the door to my front loader had been ripped off. No note no apology. But I digress. So on the Saturday I received a message to say that the company had tried to debit my credit card for $5,500 and it had failed. Well yes I wasn't sent an invoice nor advised in advance this amount which is huge for 4 nights was going to be deducted. After much arguing and toing and froing I eventually got the cost of my new machine taken off the invoice. It has ruined my holiday and I'm more stressed than ever. To summarise this is a company. They are not a charitable institution. They are in it to make money and that's fair enough. All I'm asking is please do not make promises that cannot be fulfilled. For everyone else. Ask questions don't take things on face value. I should have stepped up and done my own due diligence which I sorely regret not doing. This company is probably no better or worse than any other. There are many care providers out there. Find the right one for you. I didn't to my regret.
So I am editing this review. I have just seen the credit card amount they deducted to find they have charged me $99 more than the amended invoice I was sent. So guess I'm paying money towards my machine they broke. He very careful of this company. It's extremely sad to think they would amend and deduct extra without advising me. You know they can keep the money. I can put a full stop after this and move on knowing I am an honest person. The same cannot be said for this company

Eventually when I rang to ask for more respite care they booked it in and said they would ring me next day for payment.  No one got back to me so I rang  them to pay and was told the manager had decided they weren’t doing short term respite anymore!  What happened to giving two month’s notice before any changes are made?  I went on the website and what’s the first thing that comes up? “We do respite”!   I think I was singled out as an awkward customer.  I got my mother to write asking for confirmation in writing that the service was withdrawn before she paid another admin fee elsewhere. I’ve read a lot of the good reviews and wonder whether this company varies from branch to branch around the country?

SALES MGR PREYED ON OLD AND VULNERABLE GRANDMOTHER. AS PER MY REVIEW FOR HOME INSTEAD UK:
Where to start?!  My late Gran's niece caused the initial mess by sticking her nose in where it wasn't wanted, or needed, and tried to arrange alternative care for my then 95-year-old Gran behind my back (without doing any actual legwork!), even though I was dealing effectively with Gran's care already (and had done for nearly 20 years).  Furthermore, at the time of these events, Gran was being cared for in hospital but immediately prior to this had been in respite care in a care home, since she had ailments that meant she could not live at home and the outlook was permanent care in a care home, NOT home care.  I was Gran's carer/next-of-kin/POA.  The Manager of Live-In Care, Home Instead Glasgow, Stirling, Perth, Ayr and Falkirk (whom we'll call S) started visiting Gran without my knowledge to discuss live-in home care, and I only found out about these visits by chance - just in time too.  S ended up preying on Gran - a very elderly, weak and vulnerable person - directly with unethical and aggressive sales tactics, and I believe this is because Gran told S that she had money that her late husband AND late son had left her, and because she had been deemed as having mental capacity, even though she did not have the capacity to manage her own financial affairs - I dealt with this side of things as her carer/POA.  S hid behind Gran's 'capacity' which became her line of defence.  I had to step in to stop the nonsense before it went too far.  In the end, I sent a MASSIVE complaint email to the Care Inspectorate who passed it to management at Home Instead.  The Group Operations Manager sympathetically acknowledged and apologised that the situation had caused me stress and worry, and though S lied through her teeth about her conduct, he prevented S from continuing to pursue this lead.  In addition, due to my concerns about S, I had to ask that hospital staff, care home staff and the social worker alert me should they find S speaking to or visiting Gran.  SPECIFICS: S did not feel the need to get in touch with Gran's POA/next-of-kin (me), or do a financial assessment, or carry out a risk assessment at Gran's house, or have anyone witness conversations or join in with meetings, nor was she intending to have anyone witness the signing of any forms/agreements (which thankfully never happened but it was close - my Gran couldn't even see the forms!!)  S was hesitant to provide me with information when requested (financial or otherwise), and there was no information left for Gran at the hospital or care home for me to see.  From conversations with her, I don't believe Gran fully understood the costs, what having a live-in carer meant for her and her home, e.g. what service she was to receive and what changes had to be made to her home, and what privacy and security concerns this arrangement could cause.  What information I did finally receive from S was vague.  S was pushing to start the service only a week and a half after I got in touch with her (once I found out what was going on) even though Gran did NOT have an agreed discharge date from the hospital and there was NO agreed plan for her continuing care at that point.  S caused great confusion with hospital staff who thought Gran had home care arranged when she did not.  S even had the gall to ask me for a set of keys to Gran's house and sent me a Direct Debit form to complete!  I was SPEECHLESS.  I'd never even met the woman and I was to let her and who knows who else into Gran's home?!  Plus, as if all of this isn't enough to put people off, it turns out that carers are not always fully qualified to SVQ Level 3 and are often working towards this - therefore you could end up with a 'carer' who started at college yesterday.  Don't worry - you can REQUEST A FULLY QUALIFIED CARER (yes, I was actually told this). 
 Please be very wary of Home Instead and DO YOUR HOMEWORK BEFORE LETTING SOMEONE LIKE 'S' OR AN UNQUALIFIED CARER INTO YOUR FAMILY'S LIFE.   *I have emails to prove the majority of the information I have provided in this review, which were used as evidence for my complaint to the Care Inspectorate.  Other conversations with S were by phone.  None were in person

Awful Company. Avoid working for them or using them.. Have worked for this company for a while.  Terrible training, incompetent management, badly organised, under paid and erratic hours.  Don’t care one bit about their staff and employ half wits who couldn’t look after a goldfish rather than a person. Shockingly negligent - mother nearly died. Home instead are a shockingly negligent organisation and I do not recommend them.

While they employ some lovely, attentive carers, they also employ carers who don't care.

We pay for 4 visits a day to my disabled mother, including a nighttime visit, to ensure that she is helped into bed each night. Four nights ago, the carer didn't turn up and didn't tell us. My mother tried to get herself into bed and had a fall as a result. We didn't notice until the middle of the night, by which time she'd been on the floor for hours and we had to call an ambulance to help her up as I couldn't reach my sister who lives 10 minutes away. Luckily my sister eventually woke up and was able to go round and help my mother up. The camera in her room saved her life but - shockingly -  Home Instead has reported the camera to social services and had it removed.

What happened was that some of the carers were closing my mother's bedroom door - making it virtually impossible for her to get out of the room in the night with her walker. Other carers were not ensuring she went to the loo at night, meaning she would wake up in a few hours and try to get up to use the loo - she had a couple of falls as a result. Other carers were leaving the light on at night, even though we had asked them to switch it off - my mother would wake up in the night and think it was morning and try to get up - she had a fall as a result.

We noticed all this because of the camera and asked the carers if they would leave the door open, switch off the light and ensure she used the loo before bed. One or more of the carers didn't like that someone was "telling them now to do their job", so they retaliated by complaining that we had a camera in her room and had it removed. 

The worst thing was that they did this without giving us any notice meaning we didn't have time to put other monitoring devices into place. This means that we can no longer see if she's had a fall in the night. So tonight we are not able to look after her properly since they only switched it off at 8pm.

You can reach your own conclusions about this company based on our experiences

FOR US, getting YOUR MONEY is personal.. *AVOID* - As other have said they charge for 4 weeks of non-care if you cancel despite hospital admission, death or sudden care-home requirements (all highly likely). This amounts to £10s of thousands for some. This criminal cash-grab on the vulnerable must be made illegal and limited to two weeks at most. Many other more legitimate companies offer 24 hour cancellation for more experienced care; and I strongly recommend you go with these others; don't fall for the flashy website and 'for us it's personal' frontage. Visiting Angels or Visiting Demons?. I am writing this from a CAREGIVER'S perspective. I have been a registered nurse for over 25 years, and just signed on with this company for parttime, something to do work. 
In the 6 weeks that I was employed by an Austin Texas franchise owned by Sue Guerrero, I became appalled and frightened at the unprofessionalism practiced first by the franchise owner, SUE GUERRERO, borders upon homicidal.
She hires "caregivers" without having thoroughly vetting them , in some cases not even background checking them or even asking for identification. 
I was shocked that I was hired sight unseen , over the phone, not even having to present my nursing license. I was sent DIRECTLY to a patients home for 4 days in a row before I even met her in person.
I thought that to be suspect.
The other caregivers that I worked with were incompetent to say the least, dangerous in fact. I would NEVER leave one of my loved one's under this company's care.
On the other hand, the first two weeks I was paid fairly promptly, albeit less than what we had agreed upon. I was supposed to be paid 22.00 hourly. My check reflected 14.00 and hour. Mind you , this is with no breaks and no lunch and paying gas and car maintenence.
The second pay period , I received my check 7 days late. Today is May the 6th. Payday was the 1st. 
I have been three times to her office which is 55 minutes away because I was told to PICK UP MY CHECK. 
Each time that I have been there, her office is shut down, locked and she refuses to answer my calls.
I am a very conscientious , punctual individual who cares about her patients. I have done nothing to deserve this treatment.
Please be aware that your caregiver, althouhg you, in good conscious pay Visiting Angels in a timely manner, may not be getting paid...........

Thank you and God Bless

Nurses were great .. but special thanks the most caring element of all the - personal care assistant my total thanks for their help & comfort &  pep talks that kept his fluctuating moods positive.. it did not end how i imagined nothing does ..that id comfort him be right by his side & not be alone. But they cared & did their best… yes i would do it again using their service

Do Not Trust This Company With Your Health. Twice now, Capsule has delivered Schedule II controlled medication with missing pills. The first time I received 27 pills instead of 30 and, rather than taking responsibility, they insited the mistake was mine and that they could not replace the missing pills.  I explained that I do not need the pills replaced, am meticulous with my medications and that the correct amount had not been delivered, but they essentially shrugged their shoulders.

Two months later, it happened again. This time I received 29 pills instead of 30 and, once again, I was assured that I was mistaken. Refusing to be gaslit a second time, I asked how they could explain tablets that were never delivered. I was told their Pharmacy Manager would contact me.

After 22 days and multiple follow-ups, Pharmacy Manager Mel finally called. He asked me to explain what had happened and, after I spent all of 15 seconds doing so, sat in complete silence. Eventually I asked, “Are you still there?” His response: “Sorry, I didn’t know if I was allowed to speak.”

It felt less like discussing a serious issue with a Pharmacy Manager and more like speaking with a pouting, immature 12-year-old. I jokingly asked whether I should say “over” at the end of each sentence, to which he replied that it “would be very helpful so I know when to speak.” He then offered to transfer my prescription elsewhere. The entire conversation lasted less than 90 seconds.

The lack of professionalism and seriousness on display was astonishing, especially considering we were discussing repeated pill count discrepancies involving a Schedule II controlled substance.

I would strongly urge people not to trust this company with their health. They cannot count to 30, they refuse to take responsibility when errors are raised, and their own Pharmacy Manager lacks the maturity to handle a basic conversation. If they cannot reliably count pills, why should anyone trust them to ensure the correct dosage or even dispense the correct medication?

Most concerning of all, there is still no explanation for the missing tablets. Did Capsule make a counting error? Were there failures in their controls? I still don’t know, because nobody at the company appears interested in finding out. Capsule seemed far more concerned with transferring my prescription elsewhere than investigating why this happened twice.

Your health and wellbeing are too important to place in the hands of people who treat these issues with such alarming indifference

Cancellation without explanation. Extremely frustrating experience with Capsule Pharmacy. I received a notification that my prescription was ready for checkout, which understandably led me to believe the medication was available and my order was moving forward. Less than two hours later, I received the following message:

"Capsule here with an update on your recent order. Unfortunately, your medication is unavailable from our supplier and we do not have an availability date. We cannot transfer this prescription to another pharmacy due to state regulations and recommend you ask your doctor to send this prescription to another pharmacy."
So after telling me my prescription was ready, Capsule canceled the order and essentially left me to start the process over elsewhere. There was no explanation for how a prescription could be marked as ready and then suddenly become unavailable within hours, and no effort to provide an estimated timeline or alternative solution.
The delivery experience is also disappointing. Delivery windows are often long and inconvenient, and same-day delivery is almost never available despite being a major selling point of the service.

Customer service only adds to the frustration. Most communication happens through vague text messages that provide little useful information and make it difficult to get clear answers. When you're dealing with important medications, reliability and transparency matter. Unfortunately, Capsule failed to provide either in this situation.

I would not recommend relying on Capsule for time-sensitive prescriptions

Nope…. I used to love capsule and would recommend it to everyone I knew, BUT NO MORE!

Here are the last three things they did to mess up my last three orders.

1. I needed a medication refill for an upcoming trip. I schedule the delivery a week in advance of my trip because I knew capsule had slowed down a bit recently. Who knew even a week wouldn’t be enough time + I never got the medication. Capsule allowed me to place an order for two days later. Hours before my delivery is supposed to come, they cancel and say they don’t have the medication in stock?? I ask when they will have it and they say they have no clue. Frustrated, I ask for them to transfer it to another pharmacy. I transfer it to a cvs and they say I now have to pay the out of pocket price because capsule already ran my insurance for it and since it’s a controlled substance it can only be filed for insurance every 30 days. So not only did I not get the medication before my trip, I’m still waiting on it since it costs so much without insurance.

2. I have another trip coming up and it requires malaria medication. My doctor sent the prescription and then my travel dates changed which meant I needed more medication. I asked capsule what I should do. 1) ask my doctor for a new prescription or 2) place the first order for 16 pills and then the second for 2 pills. Stunningly they said option 2 and stood by it despite my questioning. I ordered the 16 pills and then went to order the 2. Of course it wouldn’t allow them to be delivered at the same time but even worse, they charged me $15 for the extra two pills??? And the 16 pills were only $20. Unimaginably frustrating. I will concede that they apologized and offered me a half price override, but I still paid a markup for their mistake.

3. I placed an order for antibiotics (which you need to take soon after being prescribed). Luckily, there was a rare same day delivery window. When the delivery window came, I checked the app. They RESCHEDULED my delivery for 24 hours later without notice?? I messaged them and they said the medication was out of stock so they moved it to the next available delivery window, and refused to make an exception to deliver it in the earlier window. If I had known this would happen, I would have gone to pick it up. But of course I don’t learn about it until they have closed and it’s no longer possible to pick it up (at 5 pm I might add).

If you’ve read this far thanks for listening, and I’m sorry you’ve stumbled upon what has become the worst pharmacy. Fool me once, shame on you; fool me twice, shame on me. Fool me three times…✌🏾

They are having the same attitude and now that I’m asking that the prescription be transferred, they’re again not making it any easier on me. They want me to call the new pharmacy to have them call capsule to get it going. That’s about an hour+ of wait time! I’ve already sent them the phone number and address. We will never be using this service again and will tell everyone that will listen to never use them either

I usually do not leave complaints on…. I usually do not leave complaints on public forums, but I feel compelled to warn others about serious problems with this pharmacy that may effect your health. They will admit to their error, but then tell your insurance company you lost your pills so they'll cover a new batch or blame your doctor for not providing the right information. It may be convenient to receive your medication by delivery and schedule online, but at what cost? I've sadly decided to leave - their amazing reps and marketing has kept me for long enough

Extremely disappointed. I have been a client of Alto Pharmacy for approximately three years. I manage multiple medications, and there has not been a single issue that they have been unable to resolve. Their messaging system is exceptionally user-friendly, and their response times are commendably swift. I hold this pharmacy in high esteem.

However, I received a notification yesterday indicating that they would cease supplying my Schedule II medication, which they have provided for nearly two years. The notice did not include an explanation for this discontinuation, and as a 72-year-old individual, I particularly valued the convenience of their medication delivery service, as I prefer to avoid unnecessary travel. I am quite distressed by their decision to discontinue this particular medication, necessitating my search for an alternative pharmacy. I prefer to consolidate all my medications in one location.

At this juncture, I am contemplating whether to continue my relationship with Alto Pharmacy, given that they did not provide any rationale for the cessation of this medication. The absence of an explanation in their correspondence is concerning. This experience is unprecedented for me, as I have never encountered a pharmacy that selectively determines which medications to fill. I am deeply disappointed in their service

There’s no way to directly contact your delivery driver, so you have to go through support, but the chat takes hours to respond and the hold time for calls is typically about an hour. 

It seems like Alto has lost sight of the fact that people rely on their medications, and in some cases, it could be a matter of life and death. I have lost countless hours dealing with issues and attempting to contact support. I hate to go through the hassle of changing pharmacies, but unfortunately, Alto has left me no choice. I just can’t trust them anymore and I can’t afford to have my life throw into disarray over and over due to their negligence. 

If you are reading this and have life altering prescriptions (heart medication, insulin, scheduled meds, etc), I am urging you, please look elsewhere for RX delivery. Do not put yourself at risk. Alto may be fine for routine, non-urgent prescriptions, but if you need reliable service, do not use

You said: athenahealth portal is garbageYou are far from alone in this. Widespread user reviews—from patients and clinical staff alike—frequently criticize the Athena Healthcare, Inc. Reviews on Trustpilot and the ATHENAHEALTH Reviews on Yelp for glitchy interfaces, broken login loops, and frustrating automated support.Navigating the platform's clunky design and technical limitations can be incredibly frustrating. Consider these practical workarounds to manage your healthcare needs and bypass the portal:Bypass Login Loops: If the web portal is freezing or giving you an "email not in database" error, try clearing your browser's cache, use an incognito window, or use a different web browser like Chrome, Edge, or Firefox. (A comprehensive list of supported web browsers is detailed in the Patient Portal Frequently Asked Questions - Athenahealth).Contact the Front Desk Directly: Because AthenaHealth is an enterprise software provider, their direct support is geared toward clinic administrators rather than patients. If you are locked out of your medical records or need an urgent prescription refill, call your local doctor's office directly and ask them to manually push a document or reset your registration link on their end.Avoid the Online Check-In Forms: Many users report receiving automated text/email links from the system that glitch when trying to submit paperwork online. If the system is unresponsive, you can usually just ignore the digital link and ask to fill out the paperwork in the waiting room when you arrive

A patient's pov. From a patient pov:  
I pay my bills promptly via online banking thru a major bank, and 99% of the time the provider gets a paper check w/in 5 business days. I have two providers who started using Athena. I would get constant (like daily) email a/o text nags for payment that looked like they came from the provider for any new charge but were sent by Athena.  At first I responded to nags by emailing back saying, "Paid!" w/bank payment record attached - but the "Payment due!!" nags kept on coming. I tried opening an Athena account to stop the nags but found Athena's software so dysfunctional and its privacy policy so mercenary, I returned to online banking bill pay.  
It took several complaints directly to providers to make the nags stop.
Since the dunning emails appear to come directly from the provider, Athena's obnoxious behavior makes it look like providers assume their patients are deadbeats - not a good look, esp. for psychological services. 
Athena's obnoxious, aggressive billing tactics reflect badly on providers already tarred by a predatory, broken healthcare system. Providers: You can do better

Transparency & Truthfulness Matters!. Transparency & Truthfulness Matters!
Especially if you are trying to procure new clients for a high-priced system. 
I approached Athenahealth EHR for a Demo, following which I received a call from a company intake person that took all the info re: our practice, our requirements etc. over almost 30 min on the phone. I was led to believe that the next email I would get would be to schedule the demo.
Following the initial detailed intake phone call, I received an email asking me to schedule another meeting with "senior sales analyst" to "Discuss your practice – what’s going well, where there are challenges, what goals you have for the practice/Review athena’s unique partnership model/Discuss and establish next steps" 

I courteously declined the meeting in reply to email courteously and very clearly explaining the reason for my refusal and my expectation: I had already discussed my needs and my practice specifics and so it would simply be a waste of my time providing the same information all over again not to mention  duplication of information and requested a live demo instead where I could actually see the functionality of the system. This is the industry standard for most EHR companies I have contacted------an initial intake phone followed by a demo setup. 

2 days after I declined the follow-up phone call I get a call in the morning, in the middle of a patient session from their company representative assuring me that they could schedule a demo meeting that afternoon along with the pricing modules made available at the same time if I was agreeable. I scheduled the meeting based on this reassurance.

Come the time for the meeting, first off, the salesperson tells me that he's "not able to hear me!!!", in spite of the system and browser showing repeatedly that the microphone was working just fine." Let's just a few things discuss over the phone and then I can show you the practical side of it on the desktop. We probably won't need the voice function so much for the demo!!!", he says. And guess what? The guy just had me elaborate and repeat all the intake info over the next 45 minutes. THERE WAS NO DEMO. THERE WAS NO DISCUSSION/EXPLANATION OF ANY PRICE MODULES. And when I asked re: moving on to the demo, here is his answer: "Oh, looks like we have already used up almost an hour, so there's really no time left for the demo!! And any way this was a meeting more to know about your practice. I'm going to email you some demo videos so you can review them at your convenience!!"

Really? You wasted an hour of my time, lying to me and leading me on to attend this "DEMO" meeting, just to tell me that you were chatting with me and that I could review your emailed "demo videos?" (half of which I probably wouldn't even understand without an in-person explanation of the different functionalities). No wonder, you couldn't "hear me." 
And then to top it off, he tells me that I would need to share my practice financials in order for them to give me "the most accurate, practice specific quote", and "we can definitely schedule another meeting to go over that". Seriously???!!!

So, NO DEMO & NO PRICING MODULES!!!! Just lies and waste of time. 

So, AthenaHealth, let me tell you how to impress and procure new clients: You impress and procure new clients by NOT LYING TO THEM, by respecting their time constraints, by honoring your word and by NOT LEADING THEM ON.  You do not get new clients by the shabby and deplorable behavior your employees demonstrated. This is the worst customer service that I've had the misfortune to come across. Lying and tricking your prospective clients may work in the short run to get them in to your "meetings" but that is no way to create a good impression. Your company may be "the most used EHR company" but it is the worst in customer service. 

I would neither recommend you nor your services to any of my colleagues. 

Extremely, extremely angry and never-to-be-a-client, 
Rita U. Goradia, MD

Catastrophic Experience With @athenahealth – Small Practices Beware. I signed with @athenahealth in July 2024 after months of demos and referrals. We went live November 19th. Since then, my clinic has endured one of the most disruptive, financially damaging, and exhausting experiences of my career.

Claim Failures:

No claims sent for the first 6+ weeks due to providers not configured to sign notes.

Lab billing delayed more than 10 weeks; even now Athena forces a modifier that causes denials.

DME billing delayed 16+ weeks; workflow still broken.

Prior authorizations not stored — every claim must be corrected manually.

Chiropractic Medicare claims require manual entry of initial treatment date on every visit.

Even today, 5 payers’ claims still cannot be sent.


False Promises:

Sales claimed all lab integrations → only LabCorp, and delayed.

Claimed chiropractic macros → none exist.

Claimed care plan countdowns → don’t exist.

Claimed supplement/product sales with tax → only possible by billing fake insurance claims ($6–$7 fee each).


Patient Harm:

Patients charged random amounts due to faulty care plan setups.

Incorrect statements mailed, emailed, and texted.

Lost patients due to destroyed trust.

Others constantly complain about redundant reminders.


Operational & Financial Chaos:

Fee schedule uploaded 3 times, leaving erroneous codes.

Sales tax calculated by hand.

Nearly 100 support cases opened, with hours on calls and little resolution.

Timely filing denials piling up because Athena delays triggered deadlines before claims were even sent.

$15,000 paid for an AI workaround just to survive.

Athena now demands $1,000–$3,000/month for API access to my own data.


Recordings: Athena refuses to release sales/onboarding recordings they promised were kept “for quality purposes,” likely to avoid proof of misrepresentation.

👉 Bottom line: If you are a small or independent practice, do not sign with @athenahealth. The cost — financial, operational, and emotional — is catastrophic.

#Healthcare #MedicalPractice #IndependentPractice #EMR #Athenahealth #HealthIT #PracticeManagement #PatientCare

I'm a business owner and opted to use Athenahealth's services based on attractiveness of the clinical platform and access to HIE within the EMR. I figured that we have a biller who does our billing and no EMR/PM system could mess that up.

Man, was I wrong. Athenahealth managed to be both absurdly incompetent and paternalistic toward our practice, setting up unnecessary and time-consuming requirements that only prevented us from billing and getting paid. Case in point: our biller resubmitted claims for a major payer SEVERAL times. We were nine months behind on getting actual $ from this payer. Athenahealth kept blocking the claims for pointless reasons. Finally, we broke our contract and submitted the claims through Office Ally. The claims were accepted on the first try. This is just one example of the miserable incompetence that defines Athenahealth.

We switched EMR/PM systems after about 11 months with Athenahealth, and of course it was a huge hassle for everyone including the providers, but it was worth it, and we're in better shape now. I hope this review can help someone avoid this type of experience.

No transparency, refusal to use email for communications, and critical information left out. Our experience with Tebra has been extremely disappointing and has negatively impacted the launch of our new practice.

During the sales process, we were encouraged to move forward with what was presented as an opportunity to "try out" the platform. What was not clearly disclosed was that signing the agreement effectively committed us to paying for an entire year, regardless of whether the platform met our needs. Had this been presented more transparently, we would have made a different decision.

Since realizing we were contractually obligated, we invested significant time completing Tebra University trainings. Despite completing the available education, we still found the platform difficult to navigate, and many of the questions critical to our practice operations were not addressed in the training materials.

We repeatedly requested information via email regarding onboarding requirements and billing setup. Instead, we were continuously told that the information could only be provided during an onboarding call. Scheduling those calls proved difficult because available appointment times rarely aligned with our schedules. Requests for email communication were repeatedly declined.

When we finally completed the onboarding session—more than two months after our initial demo—we were informed that additional steps were required before we could bill Medicare and Medicaid and that there would be an approximate 35-day delay even after credentialing. This information was essential to our business planning and could have been communicated in a simple email on day one. Had it been disclosed earlier, we could have begun those processes immediately rather than losing valuable time and revenue.

Perhaps most frustrating was the lack of accountability. At no point did anyone acknowledge how these delays affected our practice or offer meaningful solutions. Instead, the responsibility was largely placed on us for not being able to attend onboarding meetings that could have been replaced with basic written communication.

We have also received invoices that do not appear to align with the pricing discussed during the sales process, creating additional concerns regarding transparency.

For a company that markets itself as a solution for healthcare practices, the overall experience has felt unnecessarily complicated, poorly communicated, and lacking in professionalism. The delays and lack of transparency have cost our startup valuable time and income during a critical growth period.

Based on our experience, I would not recommend Tebra to new practices seeking a straightforward, transparent, and supportive EHR and billing solution. There are alternative platforms that, in our opinion, provide a more user-friendly experience and clearer communication

Reposting after Trustpilot removed my…. Reposting after Trustpilot removed my legitimate review under pressure from Tebra. I have the emails & reciepts!  

I ran a cash-based practice on Tebra, formerly Kareo, for nearly five years. The sales process is where they shine. The demos are polished, the promises are impressive, and none of it reflects the reality of daily use.
Patient appointment notifications were unreliable at best and completely absent at worst. I submitted support cases asking why patients were not receiving confirmations. Those cases sat unanswered for months. My patients missed appointments because the system failed to communicate with them and no one at Tebra could explain or fix it.
When I needed support for an actual clinical workflow issue, there was no one. Customer service for anything touching real practice operations does not exist. Cases opened sat for months without resolutions.
The telehealth feature was not a clinical tool. It was a checkbox on a marketing page.
When I cancelled and moved to a competitor at one-third the cost, Tebra billed me for an additional month citing a 30-day notice requirement that did not exist in my original Kareo contract. That is not a policy. That is THEFT.
After I left and posted my honest experience, Tebra had the audacity to contact me asking me to remove the review. When I refused, they went to Trustpilot. Trustpilot emailed me. I responded confirming this was a legitimate review based on nearly five years of documented experience. They removed it anyway.
That sequence of events raises a serious question about what Trustpilot actually is. If a platform emails a reviewer asking them to confirm authenticity, receives confirmation, and still removes the review after a paying advertiser applies pressure, they are not a review platform. They are a reputation management service for companies that can afford to use them.

I cannot name a single strength of Tebra. I have receipts & email communications for everything I have stated here. Providers deserve better. There are better systems. Find one

I have been a Tebra customer since before it was Tebra, when it was still Kareo. Five years of premium payments for a system that routinely failed at its most basic functions. Appointment notifications that did not work despite correct setup and repeated escalations. Forms that failed to submit. Claims that did not process. Billing issues that sat unresolved for weeks because their customer service model is built on attrition. You call, they tell you to email. You email, you hear nothing. You call again, you get someone new who cannot help you and starts the cycle over. These are clinical and financial issues. A week of silence from a vendor managing your scheduling and billing is not acceptable in any industry, and it is dangerous in healthcare.
On top of the dysfunction, they nickel and dime you for every additional feature while refusing to be accountable for the core services you are already overpaying for.
I have the invoices. I have the support tickets. I have the communication records. Every word of this is documented.
If Trustpilot removes this review, they are telling you exactly who they work for, and it is not the consumer. Tebra pays to look reputable. Do not be fooled by their rating on this platform.I do not leave reviews lightly. Run.

I rarely leave negative reviews. I rarely leave negative reviews, but this EHR system has been one of the most frustrating platforms I have ever worked with in healthcare administration. What should streamline workflows instead creates constant inefficiencies, billing delays, and unnecessary staff burden.

The user interface is outdated and incredibly unintuitive. Simple tasks require multiple unnecessary steps, pages freeze regularly, and reporting functionality is extremely limited unless you manually manipulate data outside the system. For a program that is supposed to support healthcare operations, it consistently slows them down.

The billing side is especially problematic. Claims management lacks transparency, payment posting workflows are clunky, and troubleshooting issues often feels impossible because error messaging is vague or nonexistent. Staff spend more time trying to work around the software than actually completing productive work.

Customer support has also been disappointing. Response times are slow, issues frequently go unresolved, and the answers provided often feel scripted rather than genuinely helpful. Critical problems impacting operations should not take days or weeks to address.

For the amount organizations invest in EHR systems, there should be a significantly higher standard for functionality, reliability, and support. Unfortunately, this platform has created more operational stress than efficiency for our team

Great product but difficult and inefficient customer care. I would give Tebra five stars for the platform itself—the product is strong overall. That said, the practice management side is fairly clunky and could use improvement, so I would rate the product a 4 out of 5.

Where Tebra significantly falls short is customer service.

Over the past six months alone, I’ve spent approximately 30–50 hours working through both small and large issues with support. The process is highly redundant, time-consuming, and often lacks efficient resolution. There have also been instances where unauthorized changes were made to my account by customer care representatives, which then required additional time on my end to identify and fix. I’ve also had to correct multiple financial-related issues that originated on the support side.

Because of this, I would rate Tebra a 2 out of 5 overall.

If customer care were more efficient, reliable, and did not introduce additional errors, this would easily be a 4–5 star platform. The product has strong potential, but the support experience is currently a major limitation

I have been a Tebra (formerly Kareo) customer for 9 years and used their platform as the EMR and billing system for my physical therapy clinic in Phoenix, AZ.

In November 2025, I notified Tebra that our clinic would be transitioning to a cash-pay model starting January 1, 2026. Because insurance claims take time to finalize, we arranged with their team to downgrade our services for several months so we could close out remaining claims before canceling the platform completely. This downgrade and the new monthly rate were confirmed with their team.

However, beginning in January 2026, Tebra started charging thousands of dollars above the agreed rate for services we were no longer using.

After attempting to resolve the issue and disputing one incorrect charge, Tebra suspended our account overnight and locked us out of our entire system without warning or communication.

This system contains patient records and billing data that are critical to operating a healthcare practice, and the sudden lockout caused significant disruption to our business.

After nearly a decade as a loyal customer, the lack of communication and accountability has been extremely disappointing. Healthcare practices rely heavily on these systems, and sudden account suspension during a billing dispute is incredibly concerning.

I strongly encourage other healthcare practice owners to carefully review billing practices and service agreements before relying on this platform

A Warning to Practices: DrChrono’s "Exit Traps" and Support Barriers. If you are considering DrChrono, you need to be aware of what happens when you decide to leave—because they make the process intentionally difficult, expensive, and technically exhausting.

1. Predatory Terms and Billing
-Unilateral Term Changes: They change their online terms without notification. Be warned: simply paying your regular bill constitutes "agreement" to whatever new terms they’ve posted.

-Cancellation Penalties: If you stop payment due to dissatisfaction, they consider it "damages" and will attempt to bill you for the entire remainder of your contract term.

2. The Support Gauntlet
-Phone Support is Non-Existent: Their IVR system is programmed to hang up on you if you’ve been waiting for ~5 minutes. It does not save your place in line; it simply terminates the call.

-Gatekeeping Account Managers: Only an Account Manager can cancel a contract, but you cannot be transferred to one. You must use the Chat tool, which uses "Urgent" as a de-escalation word that carries no actual weight for follow-up.

-Canned Responses: Account Managers appear to have "solve goals" rather than "help goals." Expect generic, canned responses even when you provide detailed evidence of issues. We were flatly told, "You're not eligible to leave our service," despite our documented grievances.

3. The Data Export Nightmare
-When you finally manage to leave, don't expect a clean transition.

-The "survey": Your data request must go through a Typeform survey sent to an Account Manager. There is no ticket number or confirmation—it simply goes into a database with no way to track the approval status.

-Data Fragmentation: You won't receive a cohesive database. You will receive hundreds of individual CSV files (we received over 100 for demographics alone).

-Manual Reconstruction Required: To get your patient records back, you will have to manually map "Client IDs" to "Appointment IDs" across multiple files to piece visit notes back together. It is a massive technical burden for any practice.

Bottom Line: DrChrono may work for you initially, but they have built a system designed to hold your data and your bank account hostage the moment you are no longer satisfied. Proceed with extreme caution

Paper Charts Would Be an Upgrade. It was great when we first started using it in 2018. Since EverHealth took over, it has steadily declined to the point where it’s difficult to recognize it as the same product.

The new interface is a major step backward. It’s cluttered, unintuitive, and clearly not designed with real clinical workflows in mind. Tasks that used to be quick and efficient now take significantly longer due to unnecessary steps and poor navigation. Instead of supporting the user, it consistently gets in the way.

Performance has also deteriorated. The system is slow, laggy, and often unresponsive at the worst possible times. In a clinical setting, where efficiency and reliability are critical, this creates constant friction throughout the day.

Customer support is another major issue. When problems arise, responses are delayed or unhelpful, and there’s little sense of urgency or ownership. It feels like support has become an afterthought rather than a core part of the service.

What’s most disappointing is that this platform used to work well. The current version feels overcomplicated, under-supported, and increasingly out of touch with the needs of its users.

At this point, even going back to paper charts would feel like an upgrade in terms of reliability and efficiency—which is not something anyone expects to say about modern software.

If you’re evaluating EHR options, I would strongly recommend looking elsewhere

I’ve used DrChrono for four years. I’ve used DrChrono for four years. The experience has steadily worsened.

Very poor exprience. Our organization transitioned to DrChrono expecting a stable, enterprise-level EHR suitable for a multi-provider medical practice. While reporting functionality exists, it is not intuitive. Data that appears straightforward, such as scheduled patient activity, often requires additional, non-obvious steps to be captured accurately in reports, making routine KPI tracking inefficient.

Billing stability required nearly a year of repeated troubleshooting. Even now, the system can behave inconsistently—clinical entries such as CPT codes or diagnoses may not save properly, disappear, or duplicate, and information that appears correct during documentation may display differently once routed to billing.

Patient statements are also not patient-friendly. They resemble internal accounting ledgers rather than clear, understandable statements, requiring staff to manually recreate statements upon request, adding unnecessary administrative burden.

Support has generally been courteous, but issue resolution has been slow, with timelines frequently shifting. We would also advise practices to carefully review contract terms, as some provisions are non-standard, including continued provider licensing charges even after an associate is no longer with the organization.

Overall, the platform has required significant workarounds and staff time to maintain basic operational clarity. We recommend that practices thoroughly test workflows and review contractual details before committing

Billing Dispute and Service Discontinuation Concerns. We used DrChrono as the EHR platform for our medical practice beginning in January 2023 and ultimately decided to part ways due to persistent billing and support issues.

During our time on the platform, we were charged a per-provider fee even after a provider was no longer employed or using the system. We raised this issue multiple times over several months and requested available remedies to align fees with actual provider usage, but were told adjustments could not be made due to renewal timing.

After formally disputing the charges and attempting to resolve the matter directly, our account was referred to collections while the charges remained disputed. Platform access was discontinued during a period when insurance receivables were delayed due to a government shutdown, which created additional operational challenges for a primarily Medicare-based practice.

After nearly two years on the platform, this experience made it difficult to resolve billing concerns in good faith or exit the relationship cleanly. Practices considering DrChrono, particularly those with a high Medicare patient mix, should carefully review contract terms related to provider counts, renewals, access to data, and dispute resolution before committing
