Don't be DUPED!. I chose Dr. Chrono because I needed an EHR to get me across the country (a lot less expensive than HIPAA compliant mover of paper charts).  My expectation after the demo was vastly different than what was delivered.  I've used it for four (mostly) excruciating months. 
Some might say - buyer beware - so I'll lay out a few things that were either not true, or just frustrating. 
1) Tele-medicine through Chrono is limited to 80 hours a MONTH.  I have 100% Telehealth practice and they knew that in the demo call. They will charge you an additional fee if you need more hours...significantly more than a Zoom Pro annual subscription. 
2) Onboarding is scripted and when you ask a question, they have trouble moving away from the script. 
3) Patients do not know when they have a message from OnPatient - no text available for this.
4) You will likely have to create your own note templates which are clunky and takes a significant amount of time to learn how to do them.  It took me just under a week and multiple videos that didn't explain what was needed.  So much wasted time doing this - it could have been much more streamlined. 
5) Superbill template cannot be altered and requires a signature which leads to needing another product to complete it.  It was not useful - I made my own. 
6) Online "help" documentation says it's updated, but there is no way they actually look at it.  The images they use are old and don't always related to the verbiage which is incomplete or unclear. There is a "was this article helpful" button at the bottom.  Out of 25-50 articles I read, maybe two had more positives than negatives. 
7) Clicking - clicking - clicking - click - click - click - click - click - clicking - clicking - click - and - click.
8) Feedback - they don't ask... well, at least not until I'd decided to look elsewhere, so I followed the link to the survey.  You have to sign up for a Microsoft account in order to participate.  
9) Stripe is NOT integrated.
10) Bright point was when I used a patient's phone number in "search" and their name actually came up.  That was it.  
Bottom line - Dr. Chrono is clunky, overpriced for what they ACTUALLY deliver and very far from being remotely intuitive. Save yourself the hassle - look elsewhere

If I can save just 1 clinic from this nightmare company!. I had purchased a retiring Docs clinic who was using DrChrono. Part of the agreement when purchasing the clinic was that I had to finish his contract that had approximately 6 months left on it. I called DrChrono and explained that I dont us their software and would just like to finish paying out the ritiring docs contract. They would not allow me access to the patient files in order to upload them to my EHR unless I signed an assignment agreement with them which I reluctantly signed. The staff at DRChrono was fully aware of the situation and that I did not use their software and had no plans whatsoever to use their software after the remaining contract was fulfilled. Well, the 6 months passed and DRChrono renewed my agreement for another year. I called to let them know that there had been a mistake and that it was well known to the staff that I don't use their software, never have and that I was only finishing out the retiring doctors contract. They took this matter to their managers who gave absolutely zero cares. They explained that it is in the fine details of the contract and that I had not sent in written notice. They did not care whether or not I told them over the phone, even though they told me they had recordings of the call. They did not care that I don't use, nor never have used their software because its states in the fine details (which I was never provided) that they require a 30 or 60 day written notice of cancellation. They explained that they had the legal right to hold me to the agreement that I signed. I asked them if they thought it was morally right to force me to pay for a product that I dont use and their response was that I had signed the contract, and they were going to hold me to the contract regardless of whether or not I had ever used their product. So, now Im stuck paying this terrible company over $3,000 for absolutely NOTHING! I emailed back and forth with the people for days if not weeks. They dont care about anything other than taking as much money as possible from you! Learn from my lesson, RUN from this company. They are absolutely terrible!

AVOID THIS EMR AT ALL COST!!!!!!. My entire experience has been a NIGHTMARE! Firstly, I was never fully trained from day one, it has never been fully implemented. I have limped along for way too long before giving up and firing them! Secondly, I'm having the same issues as everyone else, reach out for an issue, get the first response saying they will fix it, then ZERO follow up. Thirdly, I am in the termination process and they keep restricting my ability to send/correct claims for my final wrap up. By dragging their feet they continue to collect my month to month fee, instead of allowing me to complete my termination process. Lastly, even though I followed the correct termination process and timeline, they tried to auto-renew me for another year! I would NEVER RECOMMEND THEM, It is an AWFUL company! BUYER BEWARE!!!

I am writing in regards to Dr Chrono. I am writing in regards to Dr. Chrono.we’ve had them for over two years and we’ve had nothing but trouble with them. We are a small town family owned business with two practitioners and we are operating on a limited budget. When I decided to change services and hire an in-house coder and biller. she went back through charts,that, Chronos, coding and billing service and found several thousand dollars worth of fraudulently billed patient charts that we had already been paid for. We had to contact those payers and arrange a repayment plan. It was advertised as a hands off coding and billing service. Had we not manually audited all of their charts our practice could’ve been in serious trouble, I also received several repeated monthly billing statements that were overcharged. Once we recognized the fraudulent billing practices, we made the transition to a new EMR service Dr. Chrono immediately shut off access to our patient health information, we attempted to negotiate with them regarding the balance we owed them, and the amount of money we had to pay back to payers but they refused to listen. When  we terminated our agreement with Dr. Chrono and were making our transion to our new EMR they denied us access to all of our accounts receivable from our payers and patients. Only one week into this they shut off all services including patient health information. This has caused a serious disruption in cash flow for our clinic. We are having to get attorneys involved. We had all of those issues with customer service as  the reviews have stated and difficulty with communication with an actual person. Dr. Chrono is fraudulent and unethical I am warning anyone out there who is thinking about Dr. Chrono to absolutely run the other way. And I hope that insurance payers out there Will look into this with practices that have Dr. Chrono. They are more than capable of destroying a medical practice

Overpriced EHR with limited functionality. Before signing a one-year contract with DrChrono, consider your options. I'm halfway through my contract and have decided to switch to a different EHR despite having to pay for the remaining months. DrChrono will not let you out of the contract early.

DrChrono's EHR is more expensive than other options despite its limited functionality. Basic features such as self-scheduling and appointment reminders require additional paid apps due to poor design. Onboarding patients is so frustrating that I had to hire a virtual assistant to walk them through the process. Telehealth appointments frequently have issues with black screens. DrChrono lacks basic features such as allowing patients to add their own credit card information. 

When I informed DrChrono that I would not renew my contract at the end of my current term, they immediately attempted to terminate my account with the insurance clearinghouse, which could have prevented me from getting paid. Overall, this EHR is disappointing and I regret signing the contract

Buyer beware. The actual product does not measure up to marketing promises. Migrating patient data over was a nightmare, and there were significant extra costs that were not mentioned up front. Sinking ship - don't buy this product. We have been using DrChrono since 2012.  The Ipad part is great. AVOID THEM.  SERVICE IS TERRIBLE.. I have been with DrChrono for about 10 years - a "loyal" customer.  At the time we signed on we knew it was a fairly new company in a "new world" of EHR.  The concept is great.  I created many custom templates.  The big problem, as many other reviewers have noted, is HORRIBLE SERVICE and also frequent breakdowns.  I was promised MANY times that I would have ONE contact person.  Not only did this not happen until recently, but on the occasions when someone in the past told me they WERE the contact person, a few days later they were no longer with Chrono, and I was told they do NOT offer a single point of contact for service.  They don't leave phone numbers to call unless you manage to get one from someone answering your emails.  Overall, I find the system easy to use WHEN IT WORKS, but service is so poor I regret wasting all these years trying to get good service.  Also, when they switched to a new cloud service, the up time went the H-E double hockey sticks.  Their service performance site completely misrepresents their uptime (or should I say downtimes).  Even today, the on patient portal was NOT functioning well enough to consent patients in a timely way.  It was NOT listed as having a problem.  We see this time and time again.  There are many other service issues.  I'm glad I never used their billing given what I read above.  AVOID THIS COMPANY.  They do NOT live up to the promises of the product which has great potential.  SERVICE is everything.  I don't feel that their service leadership truly understands what it means to not have good communication, rapid resolution of problems, and true customer satisfaction for a medical practice that is small.  It is highly disruptive when it goes down, and at times you can't even report a problem since doing so requires the system to be up and running!
PS:  I just read the response below.  The response is exactly what I've been told MANY MANY times over the past 10 years regarding this same issue of poor service.  Frankly, I would venture to guess  this is not a PEOPLE problem.  It is a system problem.  You really need to assign a single person (with clearly identified back up when that person is on vacation or not available) as a service point of contact that would include sales/service/etc.  The T-Mobile service model might be applied.  Ergo, the response to this is more of the same and an indication that once again the system is completely failing customers that need service.  And believe me, this is hardly the only "down" time they have, and their charts/graphs of down time don't come close to showing service problems

Scammed. In early 2019 we were scammed into paying for a year of DrChrono for a little over $2,000. We were left in the cold during implementation and the manager stopped returning our calls. No improvement with the new owner.. Update 10/2024
They have changed their help ticket format. There is no longer somewhere to view progress on all open tickets.  We had to make a spreadsheet ourselves to track ticket numbers, so we can email them and ask them for status updates. 

There is no real way to export patient files to a new EHR.  8 years of records for 8 providers will be an absolute nightmare. We’re basically hostages.

UPDATE: management did mention that they saw this review and gave me a credit on my statement. They have not, however, solved help tickets that have been open for 6+ weeks. So my assessment of them stands.  Buyer beware. Actually, don’t beware. Choose a different vendor.


We’ve been with dr chrono for almost 5 years.  The first year was really good.  And then things went steadily downhill, and the service is now abysmal.  

They do a decent job at initial onboarding, when you first sign the contract....because they still are trying to impress you.  But later on when a new provider joins your group, be prepared for several months of pulling your hair out as you try to get the new person on boarded.  Your new provider will receive no training.  YOU will have to be on DrChrono about every detail, because they certainly don’t have an onboarding punch list. Keep in mind that if a provider leaves your practice, they will keep charging you for that provider until your annual contract ends.  Even if you replace that provider. 

My newest provider has been waiting a month for them to get his credit card processing working.  This after waiting 1.5 months for them to set up his claim submission correctly. So here we are 3 months and almost $1000 in monthly fees in and he still can’t fully collect payment for his services.

What else?  Automatic appointment reminders may or may not get sent. Your note or that appointment you’re trying to make may or may not get saved.  Electronic statements that never make it to the patient portal.  Claims that give the appearance of having been sent, but never make it to the insurance company... All “customer service” is via help tickets, no live assistance.  They advertise integration with quest and labcorp, but labcorp refuses to integrate unless you’re a huge practice, so don’t count on it. Quest did eventually get integrated with us, but it took months and months and me going a little bit ballistic before it actually got done.

They recently announced that they are moving their servers to AWS, and it will solve all the problems. They’ve been saying some thing or another will fix things for years now

We only started using their system and…. We only started using their system and they have removed patient data, demographics,clinical notes,billing and appointments. The foundation is there but the  customer service is just not there. Empty promises.  Only work during the week. Ridiculous too hard to reach. They mess up the accounts with no plan to correct. Not professional
